description: RRF correctly ranks dual-modality chunks above single-modality outliers. Query 'metformin renal dosing CKD' should retrieve at least one chunk that the corpus indexes via BOTH BM25 (lexical 'metformin', 'renal') and vector embedding (semantic 'CKD' / 'creatinine clearance'). Under the prior max-score fusion, a single high-BM25 outlier could outrank chunks present in both legs because BM25 scores are unbounded while cosine scores cap near 1.0. RRF normalizes this by ranking each modality independently and summing 1/(K+rank) terms.
what_bug_this_catches: If the retriever regresses to max-score dedup (or any fusion that uses raw BM25/cosine values directly), a single high-BM25 outlier outranks chunks that appear strongly in both modalities. The dual-modality chunk still appears in the candidate list, but it ranks below the single-modality outlier, which means the reranker burns a top-K slot on a less-relevant result. RRF guarantees that any chunk appearing in BOTH legs accumulates two 1/(K+rank) contributions and therefore outranks any chunk appearing in only one leg, regardless of raw score magnitude.

input.query: metformin renal dosing CKD creatinine clearance threshold
